summary: LEGACY_ROLE_ALIASES Security Fix Verification - All 24 tests PASS. The fix correctly prevents plain 'admin' users from bypassing feature flags while preserving backward compatibility for route access.
critical_code_review_comments: is_super_admin() correctly uses _raw_roles to distinguish explicit super_admin from normalized admin
Plain 'admin' role users NO longer bypass feature flag checks (require_feature)
Explicit 'super_admin'/'superadmin' users correctly bypass feature flags
LEGACY_ROLE_ALIASES still works for require_roles() backward compatibility
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed orgs and users for isolated testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security fix for LEGACY_ROLE_ALIASES verified. Key verification points: 1) is_super_admin() checks _raw_roles (pre-normalization) - only explicit super_admin/superadmin passes, 2) require_feature() uses is_super_admin() so admin users get feature-checked, 3) require_roles() uses normalize_roles() so admin→super_admin aliasing still works for route access, 4) Live verification: agent@acenta.test (super_admin) gets 200 on /api/admin/settlements and /api/admin/whitelabel, agency1@demo.test (agency_admin) gets 404/403 on same endpoints.

security_verification.super_admin_bypasses_feature_flags: PASS - returns 200 regardless of org features
security_verification.require_roles_backward_compatibility: PASS - admin can access super_admin routes via normalization
